Related stories and ads should be ignored.

Why source fidelity matters

Research systems that compile durable knowledge bases need to know whether a capture is a complete source or a derived excerpt. A complete capture can support precise citations, later diffing, and review. A derived excerpt can still be useful, but it should carry lower confidence and be made visible before any article is written.

The article fixture exercises the article tag path rather than the main tag path. It should still extract the article body and ignore surrounding boilerplate. This verifies that the fallback converter is not tied to a single document structure.

Operational rule

Only content returned by a verbatim route can be labeled full fidelity. HTML extraction is deterministic but lossy, so it is always labeled extract. Very short client-rendered shells are labeled failed because they do not contain enough source text to ground downstream claims.

More text is included here to keep the fixture above the threshold used for shell detection. The exact words are less important than the structural contract: title, paragraphs, secondary heading, and enough body content to represent an article.
